name: Willie Humphrey
abstract: Willie James Humphrey (December 29, 1900 – June 7, 1994) was a New Orleans jazz clarinetist. Willie Humphrey was born in a musical family, the son of prominent local clarinetist and music teacher Willie Eli Humphrey; his brothers Earl Humphrey and Percy Humphrey also became well known professional musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Maurice Durand
about_quotes.evidence: Well, in Durant and Humphrey band? Mm-hmm. We had, uh, Bebe Mathis on drum. Willie, uh, Buddy Johnson, trombone, I'm playing trumpet. Willie Humphrey, clarinet.

about_quotes.speaker: Emmanuel Sayles
about_quotes.evidence: And, uh, the saxophone section was, uh, uh, let's see, Willie, uh, Willie Humphrey was in there playing, I think, third sax.

about_quotes.speaker: Lee Collins
about_quotes.evidence: Then Willie Humphrey Client, so, we mix up a band, we mix up a band together, and we played around together a long time

about_quotes.speaker: Father Al Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: Well I had Benny Allen on piano, I had Gaudet playing trumpet, Willie Humphrey playing clarinet.

about_quotes.speaker: Ricard Alexis
about_quotes.evidence: Him and I and Black Benny work together. Years, that he liked.

about_quotes.speaker: Manuel Joseph "Moose" Zanco
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Willie Humphrey only in the context of John Handy and Willie Humphrey playing together, and in relation to John enrolling Willie in a club. No direct personal interaction or relationship between the interviewee (Moose Zanco) and Willie Humphrey is described.
